title: Universal gas constant
short: The constant R ≈ 8.314 J/(mol·K) linking pressure, volume, moles and temperature in the ideal gas law.
full:
The universal gas constant R is the proportionality constant in the ideal gas law, P·V = n·R·T. Its value, R ≈ 8.314 J/(mol·K), is the same for every ideal gas — that universality is what makes the law so useful.

It ties together the macroscopic state variables (pressure, volume, temperature) with the amount of gas in moles. The calculator already substitutes R for you.

The SI unit is the joule per mole-kelvin, J/(mol·K).
